text:
Once upon an autumn evening, under a sky thick with oranges and purples, there sat an old owl.

Olivia. She loved winter candles. Especially those big ones crackling.

One day she'd leave crumbs outdoors. Ducks ate some.

Snowfall. She loved snowfall.

She'd look.

Stars.

She'd eat.

Books.



One night, she just looked upward.

Moon.



You.



She thought.

A little boat?

On the sea?

She'd row.

Wind.

Soon.

You.

She sailed.

A secret.

You.



Maybe.

Next year.

Anyway.



Every day. She'd just read.

Book.



She loved those.

Now.

Come.

She'd think.

The sea.

So big.




I'll find you.





You'll read.

Maybe.



I'll help.



She thought.



You know that.

You don.

Oh.


There.



Just one.

That.



You know.



I find you.

There.

You'd think.

It would.

Forever.



That
